# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #729 | bug(replan): empty or uncovered failed-review findings can activate a review-only child epoch | PARTIALLY SHIPPED (8370e577) — case (a) empty-frontier refusal landed and is mutation-pinned; 3 of 9 acceptance criteria met | — | SHIPPED: reviewSourceCarriesFrontier + typed refusal replan_source_findings_missing, bound at every reachable frontier (source read, authority verification, planner packet, stored-transaction entry at both phases, candidate re-author); 8/8 mutants killed; recovery row added to the repair-routing card. TWO LOAD-BEARING SCOPING CALLS to preserve in any follow-up — the guard is scoped to authority_kind review_outcome because diagnosis_to_build legitimately carries findings [] (pinned by a killed mutant), and the predicate is non-empty FINDINGS not non-empty OPEN frontier, because a progress-only failure legitimately carries an all-resolved frontier whose record of what failed is intact. REMAINING (6 of 9 ACs): AC2 planner packets carrying immutable finding ids/anchors/statuses/ownership data (buildPlannerPacket emits none today — zero hits for route_candidates/ownership_candidates/owning_node); AC3 child carry-forward reporting uncovered uid/path/node; AC4 review-only child cannot activate for an unresolved path-based finding; AC5 correctly mapped writer-to-certifier still activates; AC6 N-1 of N coverage refuses; AC7 crash-prefix/idempotent retry at the new coverage seam. Case (b) SEMANTIC COVERAGE deliberately unimplemented with the soundness argument recorded inline: every mechanical proxy at this seam is unsound (an anchor records where a defect was OBSERVED and may legally carry no path; fix_role is advisory not contractual; "child has any writer" does not even catch the live repro). A sound check needs the child plan to DECLARE which inherited finding uids each writer owns — a plan-grammar addition in the validator, so AC2 is its natural prerequisite. Minor: repairCandidateReauthorPrefix performs durable writes before the guard on one resume path (epoch is NOT consumed and phase does not advance, so AC1 holds, but "zero mutation" is not literally true there). EPIC NOTE (#757): the finding-frontier planner packet becomes the brief substrate for #761 — keep the frontier shape consumable outside the replan seam; no scope change. |
| #730 | bug(repair-dispatch): reopened fixer receives IDs but not a digest-bound canonical finding brief | ATTEMPTED AND REJECTED 2026-07-22 (adversarial review, blocks_commit) — rework required; the #727 commit it was stacked with shipped separately | — | REWORK. The rejected attempt built the brief but left the OWNERSHIP-ABSENT path telling the authorized fixer to fix NOTHING: when no open finding routes to an owner (anchor-less schema-2 evidence_observation findings, findings anchored at a file no node declares, or a pre-#701 journal), the ownership bridge stays inert and the reopen proceeds on the graph-maximal proof alone — so the reopened writer IS responsible for the whole open frontier, yet buildRepairBrief emits an empty assigned-uid set. The flagship test asserted the exact behavior the issue forbids: §1 requires that if ANY open finding is unowned, ambiguously owned, or owned by another writer, direct repair must REFUSE and route to replan rather than silently omit it. Also correct on the redo: an evidence claim was false (the walkthrough was cited as a second frontier but greps zero for repair_brief), and for schema-2 the "reviewer's verbatim statement" is vacuous — describe() prefers row.raw and schema2RouteCandidates synthesizes raw as 'uid=<uid>', while canonical findings carry no human-readable statement field at all (only failure_class / trigger digests / anchors / proof_digest), so that claim holds only for schema-1 flat rows. Keep the audited scope trim: an existing backstop (closure re-review carries prior_findings forward and refuses without a per-uid resolution_json) already re-fails the gate on an unaddressed finding, so do NOT rebuild it and do NOT add a close-time proof that merely re-verifies it. The un-backstopped core is that consumed_by is set when the selected writer owns AT LEAST ONE open finding, not all of them. EPIC NOTE (#757): the digest-bound canonical brief becomes the fixer brief for #761 — keep the brief constructor callable outside repair-dispatch; no scope change. |
| #745 | bug(plan-validator): interior code-reviewer/security-reviewer walls still false-block on disjoint downstream writes — the surface-freshness rescue covers only adversarial-verifier, and their reopen remedy still dead-ends | PARTIALLY SHIPPED — the false-block half landed (merge window ending 7d0df364); the reopen-remedy half is deliberately unimplemented | — | SHIPPED: interiorSurfaceFresh widened to code-reviewer/security-reviewer, with the producer slice RECOMPUTED from the frozen graph (not trusted from the journal) and a final certifier counting as a live whole-candidate wall only when its ledger row is actually complete. REMAINING: the reopen remedy. Auto-parking a "provably pristine" sink was REJECTED as unsafe, not deferred — the sink runs main-session-direct and its first irreversible step writes no in-band marker, so a pristine sink cannot be distinguished from one that already committed/pushed/merged. A safe remedy needs an explicit sink-progress marker. Cross-epoch freshness (seal attempt absent after journal rotation) also still falls back to whole-candidate. RESHAPED 2026-07-22 under epic #757: the reopen-remedy half is SUPERSEDED by #761 (local re-expansion; reopen becomes rollback-only) — do not implement it here. When #762 lands, re-check whether the shipped freshness widening is still load-bearing: declared+amended-surface staleness removes the whole-candidate binding at the root, and the cross-epoch residual dissolves with it. |
| #756 | bug(finalize/reopen): finalize done-gate surfacing a required file outside a completed writer's frozen write set wedges a PASSED-review serial run (reopen strands, amend orphans journal, replan needs a failed attempt) | ready — filed 2026-07-22 from vrpai-cli #1005, with all six recovery dead-ends reproduced empirically | — | RESHAPED 2026-07-22 under epic #757 — this issue is now the ACCEPTANCE HARNESS for #761 (local re-expansion: removes dead-ends 3/4/6, no failed-attempt prerequisite) + #762 (declared-not-walled surfaces: removes dead-ends 1/2/5); do NOT batch with the #745 reopen-remedy half (superseded by #761). Of the three candidate directions, direction 1 was chosen and generalized; direction 2 (tail-first cascade) is unnecessary (reopen stays rollback-only); direction 3 (completeness lint) is downgraded to an optional planner aid. A run whose review PASSED wedges at finalize when a project done-gate (not modeled as a DAG node) surfaces a required code-relevant file outside every frozen write set: barrier-check refuses write_set_overflow, verdict-check refuses verdict_not_pass (the code-relevant file stales the certifier candidate; docs correctly do not), reopen-node on the writer refuses would_strand_completed_dependent, on the gate refuses would_orphan_in_progress then would_strand, write-set amend + freeze --repair refuses review_journal_plan_hash_mismatch, and replan refuses replan_source_journal_missing because it requires a FAILED-review attempt a passed review never produces. Three candidate directions in the issue: (1) a sanctioned expand-and-re-review primitive that does not require a prior failed attempt, (2) reopen-node tail-first cascade instead of a hard would_strand refusal, (3) a planner companion-file completeness lint. Acceptance requires the recovery keep the new file REVIEWED, not merely attributed. |
| #757 | epic(adaptive): progressive elaboration — coarse frozen spine + just-in-time expansion, parallel-by-default | open — design filed 2026-07-22; children #758–#763 | — | Run children in order #758 (spine grammar) → #759 (expansion transaction) → #760 (serialization-trigger inversion) → #761 (local re-expansion) → #762 (declared-not-walled surfaces) → #763 (context packet + efficiency line) → #766 (opencode/kimi port sweep) → #765 (cutover, LAST); #761/#762 depend on #759. Per-child planning deltas live as 2026-07-22 comments on each issue (write-set inventories, serializer-taxonomy completion, finalize admissibility boundary, write-lane hook ordering). Principle prose already propagated ahead of mechanics at commit ad196273 (axioms+embeds, planner md+tomls, plan-run skeleton ×12) with chains DEFERRED — the first child shipping a cross-edition diff runs all four chains. Start only after in-flight #729/#730 land — their finding-frontier packet and digest-bound brief are #761's substrate. Migration is dual-accept (legacy full-DAG plans keep byte-identical behavior); the cutover decision is an ADR at epic close. Hard anchors that must NOT loosen: consent valve, guard prologue, atomic freeze, reviewed-before-sink wall, finalize receipts, running-set ledger atomicity, unattributed-write tamper refusal. STRATEGY (user directive 2026-07-22): evidence-backed serialization — serial requires present-tense nameable evidence for S1/S2/S3; predictions and uncertainty are inadmissible; mode is evidence-governed, width stays with faithful decomposition (see the #760 addendum). |
| #758 | feat(plan-validator): spine grammar — freeze a coarse milestone spine with typed expansion points; retire interior whole-plan shape proofs | queued — epic #757 child 1 | — | plan-validator gains the SPINE shape: ordered milestones + unique sink; nodes are concrete single-role OR expansion_point (frontier composed at open time). Spine-level proofs stay global (unique sink, sink post-dominance on the spine, reviewed-before-sink wall, closed library, caps, governance); interior post-dominance/antichain proofs are NOT applied to expansion points. Each expansion_point declares milestone goal, coarse ADVISORY expected surfaces, join constraints, and its review obligation class. plan_hash covers the spine only. Dual-accept legacy plans, regression-pinned. Known seam: review-journal binding moves to (spine hash, expansion id) in #759 — coordinate, don't solve here. DELTA 2026-07-22: write set must also carry adaptive-handoff.js (spine freeze flows through it), agents/workflow-planner.md + the three T17-byte-matched tomls (canonical = github toml), the six adapt-topic surfaces, docs/api.md, docs/workflow-state-contract.md. |
| #759 | feat(adaptive-node): expansion transaction — open-time frontier composition recorded append-only in the ledger, resume-safe | queued — epic #757 child 2 (depends on #758) | — | New adaptive-node subcommand expand-open: takes units[] {role, tier, write surfaces, mode serial\|co_open, dependency edges} + the derivation lines (grain/path/join/probe/serializer), appends an EXPANSION RECORD (append-only, monotonically numbered per point) to the ledger, then opens via the EXISTING running-set scheduler. Guard prologue applies. Crash between append and open reconciles via reconcile-running-set roll-forward (idempotent). next-action.js ready-set derivation reads spine + expansion records. Review journal rebinds to (spine plan_hash, expansion id) so re-expansion never orphans a completed journal. Re-expansion is the SAME transaction — no separate machinery. DELTA 2026-07-22: the six workflow-init surfaces are IN SCOPE (their generated CLAUDE.md describes freeze + Node Ledger + resume-reads-the-ledger) plus docs/workflow-state-contract.md and the resume/frontier-batch plan-run cards; model-tier resolution moves from next-action.js plan-lookup to units carrying tier at open time — name the single resolver owner. |
| #760 | feat(protocol): serialization-trigger inversion — co-open by default at open time; serial requires a named serializer | queued — epic #757 child 3 (depends on #759) | — | Invert the burden of proof: mode defaults to CO-OPEN in isolated per-leg worktrees; serial only on a NAMED serializer backed by PRESENT-TENSE EVIDENCE (user directive 2026-07-22) — S1 true data dependency (name the concrete artifact B consumes from A, or co-open), S2 shared irreversible external effect (name the shared resource — resource identity, not a conflict forecast), S3 no worktree support (the failed probe result). Predictions/anticipations/uncertainty are INADMISSIBLE grounds for serial. Evidence lines are audit-only derivations — the only mechanical check permitted is that a line EXISTS; no validator parses content. Governs MODE only: width stays with faithful decomposition (grain merges under-sized units). UNCERTAIN overlap is NOT a serializer (co-open isolated + mandatory synthesizer reconcile; extends the existing containment+reconcile correctness net from proven-disjoint to uncertain). Grain adjusts unit COARSENESS, never mode. --write-overlap-consent narrows to the S2 class. Propagate to the SIX routing surfaces via skeletons+slots regeneration (never hand-edit the 12 generated surfaces); zero uncertain-implies-serial language may remain; route-reachability + all four contract validators green. DELTA 2026-07-22: taxonomy completion — proven same-file overlap = MERGE the units (grain response), serial-with-named-path as fallback; KAOLA_PARALLEL_WRITES=0 survives as operator serializer (classify with S3); PROTECTED files fold into S2/consent, post-dominating-gate condition discharged by the spine review wall; rewrite surfaces include slots.js frontmatter descriptions + token pins. Posture block already in the skeleton at ad196273 — this child replaces the mechanics prose around it. |
| #761 | feat(adaptive-node): local re-expansion — any finding re-expands its owning expansion point; no failed-attempt prerequisite | queued — epic #757 child 4 (depends on #759; consumes #729 packet + #730 brief) | — | A finding from ANY source — failed review, PASSED-review candidate staleness, finalize done-gate (#756 trigger), out-of-surface attribution (#762) — routes to local re-expansion of the owning expansion point: a new expansion record with at minimum {fixer unit, re-review unit}; reviewed-before-sink preserved by construction. Fixer brief = digest-bound canonical brief (#730 shape) built from the finding frontier (#729 shape). Ownership by declared+amended surface; spanning findings re-expand each owner; a finding outside every milestone's scope is the ONLY remaining epoch-replan trigger. Retire as primary remedies: unique-maximal-producer escalation to replan for interior findings, the reopen strand/orphan family (reopen = rollback only), the replan_source_journal_missing failed-attempt prerequisite. AC: the #756 repro reaches a passing REVIEWED finalize with no hand-edits; the #745 reopen dead-end resolves. DELTA 2026-07-22: finalize re-expansion is admissible only BEFORE the sink's first irreversible step (commit/push/merge), determined from DERIVED sink-progress evidence (sink writes no in-band marker); after that it is a follow-up claim. The #729 empty-frontier refusals bind unchanged at spine-level replans. |
| #762 | feat(barrier): declared-not-walled write surfaces — append-only surface amendments with attribution; overflow becomes attribution + re-review | queued — epic #757 child 5 (depends on #759 + #761) | — | Write surfaces become declarations for attribution/review-scoping, not walls. Out-of-surface companion file → append a surface AMENDMENT to the owning expansion record + route to re-review via #761; write_set_overflow stops refusing attributable source-tree files. Certifier candidate digest/staleness computed against declared+AMENDED surfaces (invalidates exactly the re-review unit, no whole-candidate staling). HARD anchors kept: unattributed out-of-surface write at sink = fail-closed tamper refusal; consent-gated curated/irreversible roots (S2) still require the consent valve — amendment loosens attribution, never consent. AC: #756 dead-ends 1/2/5 gone in the repro; tamper case still refuses. DELTA 2026-07-22: the KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT hook denies out-of-lane writes AT WRITE TIME — the flow is attribute → amend (lane widens) → write, never write-first; amendments obey the exact-file-paths-only granularity rule (no directories/globs). |
| #763 | feat(adaptive): shared context packet + per-expansion efficiency evidence line | queued — epic #757 child 6 (anytime after #759) | — | Two lightweight mechanisms, explicitly not a subsystem. (1) Orientation emits .cache/context-packet.md (goal, constraints, key files, conventions, join expectations); every frontier unit dispatch includes it verbatim — lowers per-agent setup cost, raising profitable width under the grain rule. (2) On expansion close, ONE evidence line: expansion <id>: width=<N> mode=<serial\|co_open> serializer=<none\|S1\|S2\|S3> rework=<K>; one rollup line per run in the archive summary — makes the fan-out turning point empirical. Non-goals: no token metering, no timing infra, no dashboards, no new durable files beyond the packet. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
